title: Carbeth Plants - BlackBerry Rubus Thornless Fruit Plant in 9cm Pot - Outdoor Ready to Plant Summer Fruiting Berry Shrub - Thorn Free Child Friendly Fruit Bush - Ideal for Patio Pots, Beds and Borders
features: BLACKBERRY THORNLESS PLANT - The Rubus Thornless Blackberry plant is a low maintenance upright growing pollinator friendly, fruit shrub ideal for planting in UK gardens. This garden ready plant produces an abundance of large tasty glossy black berries every August, on stems that are thorn free, making it children friendly and safer to pick from. The sweet fruit can be used to make jams and desserts or eaten straight from the plant.

HARDY PLANT - This fruiting shrub is extremely hardy and can survive in colder climates and still bear fruit. It is ideal for growing in patio pots or in beds and borders in kitchen gardens, cottage gardens and woodland gardens. It can grow to a height of 150cm with a spread of a similar size.

SUNLIGHT - This thornless blackberry plant will happily grow in full sunlight to partial shade in any moist draining soil. This fruiting shrub will grow in a variety of soil conditions but very chalky soil will need to be bulked up with well rotted organic matter.

WATERING - When watering this fruit plant it is important to water well until it is established and especially during the hotter months of Summer. During the Autumn cut away fruited stems and tie in new stems in preparation for the next year’s crop.

Seasonal Colour & Fragrance Offers vibrant blooms or aromatic foliage that enhance borders, patios and containers throughout the year. Wildlife Friendly Attracts bees, butterflies and birds, supporting pollinators and encouraging natural biodiversity. Low Maintenance & Hardy Easy to grow, drought tolerant and resilient in varied UK climates, ideal for beginners and busy gardeners. Long-Term Structure Theres a shrub for every garden. Bring year round interest with evergreen shrubbery, or a burst of colour in the growing season with seasonal flowering hedges. When is the best time to plant shrubs? The best time to plant shrubs is in autumn or early spring when the soil is moist but not waterlogged. This allows roots to establish before hot or frosty weather. Do shrubs need pruning? Light pruning once a year helps maintain shape and encourages new growth. Prune after flowering for spring-flowering shrubs and in late winter for summer bloomers. Can I grow shrubs in containers? Yes, many compact or slow growing shrubs thrive in pots. Use a quality compost and ensure good drainage to prevent root rot. How can I encourage flowering? Plant in the correct position, feed with a balanced fertiliser in spring, and avoid over-pruning flower buds. Are shrubs suitable for wildlife gardens? Absolutely! Many shrubs provide nectar, shelter, and berries for birds and pollinators throughout the year.
